summary: Verified two new features (iter47): (1) community 'crawls survived' counter API + UI, (2) guided intro Skip button + localStorage persistence. All 5 requested scenarios PASS. Backend pytest 2/2 pass; frontend E2E used exactly 1 Google Places Deal click as instructed.
critical_code_review_comments: Home.jsx correctly reads localStorage 'ff_guided_seen' on mount with try/catch guard — safe.
GuidedFlow.jsx guided-skip button is a visible pill in the header row (top-right), reasonably prominent.
CrawlBadgeDialog.jsx POSTs /api/stats/crawl-completed on open and renders the returned count as a community line — works as designed.
Backend /api/stats/crawls GET returns {count:0} when doc missing (fallback), and POST uses find_one_and_update with upsert + ReturnDocument.AFTER — idempotent-safe, correct increment semantics.
seed_data_creation: Incremented db.stats.crawls_completed by 1 during E2E test (from 2 to 3).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend counters (fates_dealt, crawls_completed) share the same pattern in db.stats. Google Places live search cap: this run used 1 Deal click. Home page counter appears with 'N crawls survived' next to 'X fates dealt' as expected. localStorage key 'ff_guided_seen'='1' set on skip; Guide header button reopens the wizard manually.

evidence.community_line_text: 🏆 3 brave souls have survived a Fork·Fate crawl
